verses.text: At Pentecost a rushing wind fills the house, cloven tongues of fire appear, and they are 'filled with the Holy Ghost' and speak with tongues. The Spirit poured out. The promised gift descends in power.

At Pentecost a rushing wind fills the house, cloven tongues of fire appear, and they are 'filled with the Holy Ghost' and speak with tongues. The Spirit poured out. The promised gift descends in power.

At Pentecost a rushing wind fills the house, cloven tongues of fire appear, and they are 'filled with the Holy Ghost' and speak with tongues. The Spirit poured out. The promised gift descends in power.

At Pentecost a rushing wind fills the house, cloven tongues of fire appear, and they are 'filled with the Holy Ghost' and speak with tongues. The Spirit poured out. The promised gift descends in power.
Devout men of every nation hear them in their own tongue, amazed. A reversed Babel. The gospel speaks to all peoples at once.
Devout men of every nation hear them in their own tongue, amazed. A reversed Babel. The gospel speaks to all peoples at once.
Devout men of every nation hear them in their own tongue, amazed. A reversed Babel. The gospel speaks to all peoples at once.
Devout men of every nation hear them in their own tongue, amazed. A reversed Babel. The gospel speaks to all peoples at once.
They hear 'the wonderful works of God'; some mock, 'full of new wine.' Mixed response. The same wonder draws faith and scorn.
They hear 'the wonderful works of God'; some mock, 'full of new wine.' Mixed response. The same wonder draws faith and scorn.
They hear 'the wonderful works of God'; some mock, 'full of new wine.' Mixed response. The same wonder draws faith and scorn.
They hear 'the wonderful works of God'; some mock, 'full of new wine.' Mixed response. The same wonder draws faith and scorn.
They hear 'the wonderful works of God'; some mock, 'full of new wine.' Mixed response. The same wonder draws faith and scorn.
Peter stands, These are not drunken; 'this is that which was spoken by... Joel.' Scripture fulfilled. The outpouring was long foretold.
Peter stands, These are not drunken; 'this is that which was spoken by... Joel.' Scripture fulfilled. The outpouring was long foretold.
Peter stands, These are not drunken; 'this is that which was spoken by... Joel.' Scripture fulfilled. The outpouring was long foretold.
'I will pour out... my Spirit upon all flesh'; 'whosoever shall call on the name of the Lord shall be saved.' Open door. Salvation is offered to all who call.
'I will pour out... my Spirit upon all flesh'; 'whosoever shall call on the name of the Lord shall be saved.' Open door. Salvation is offered to all who call.

'I will pour out... my Spirit upon all flesh'; 'whosoever shall call on the name of the Lord shall be saved.' Open door. Salvation is offered to all who call.
Jesus, approved by miracles, ye crucified; 'whom God hath raised up,' loosing death's pains. The crucified raised. Death could not hold the Lord of life.
Jesus, approved by miracles, ye crucified; 'whom God hath raised up,' loosing death's pains. The crucified raised. Death could not hold the Lord of life.
Jesus, approved by miracles, ye crucified; 'whom God hath raised up,' loosing death's pains. The crucified raised. Death could not hold the Lord of life.
David foretold, 'thou wilt not leave my soul in hell.' Prophecy of rising. The resurrection was promised in the Psalms.
David foretold, 'thou wilt not leave my soul in hell.' Prophecy of rising. The resurrection was promised in the Psalms.
David foretold, 'thou wilt not leave my soul in hell.' Prophecy of rising. The resurrection was promised in the Psalms.
David foretold, 'thou wilt not leave my soul in hell.' Prophecy of rising. The resurrection was promised in the Psalms.
David died and saw corruption, but spoke of Christ, whose flesh saw none. Greater than David. The prophet pointed to the deathless Christ.
David died and saw corruption, but spoke of Christ, whose flesh saw none. Greater than David. The prophet pointed to the deathless Christ.
David died and saw corruption, but spoke of Christ, whose flesh saw none. Greater than David. The prophet pointed to the deathless Christ.
God raised this Jesus; exalted, He has 'shed forth this, which ye now see.' Source of the Spirit. The risen Lord pours out the gift.
God raised this Jesus; exalted, He has 'shed forth this, which ye now see.' Source of the Spirit. The risen Lord pours out the gift.
'God hath made that same Jesus... both Lord and Christ.' Exalted Lord. The crucified is enthroned as Lord of all.
'God hath made that same Jesus... both Lord and Christ.' Exalted Lord. The crucified is enthroned as Lord of all.
'God hath made that same Jesus... both Lord and Christ.' Exalted Lord. The crucified is enthroned as Lord of all.
Pricked in heart, What shall we do? 'Repent, and be baptized... for the remission of sins.' The way of pardon. Repentance and faith bring forgiveness and the Spirit.
Pricked in heart, What shall we do? 'Repent, and be baptized... for the remission of sins.' The way of pardon. Repentance and faith bring forgiveness and the Spirit.
Pricked in heart, What shall we do? 'Repent, and be baptized... for the remission of sins.' The way of pardon. Repentance and faith bring forgiveness and the Spirit.
Three thousand are added; 'they continued stedfastly in the apostles' doctrine and fellowship.' First church. New converts hold fast to teaching, fellowship, and prayer.
Three thousand are added; 'they continued stedfastly in the apostles' doctrine and fellowship.' First church. New converts hold fast to teaching, fellowship, and prayer.
Three thousand are added; 'they continued stedfastly in the apostles' doctrine and fellowship.' First church. New converts hold fast to teaching, fellowship, and prayer.
Fear comes on all; the believers share all things as any had need. Generous love. Grace overflows in open-handed sharing.
Fear comes on all; the believers share all things as any had need. Generous love. Grace overflows in open-handed sharing.
Fear comes on all; the believers share all things as any had need. Generous love. Grace overflows in open-handed sharing.
Continuing daily with gladness, praising God; 'the Lord added... daily such as should be saved.' Growing church. Joyful worship draws more to Christ.
